nodes.label: HTML (HyperText Markup Language)
nodes.author: Sagar Thapa
nodes.rationale: Standard markup language defining structure, content, and semantic meaning of web documents; serves as the universal interface between server-side logic and client-side presentation, providing standardization, accessibility, SEO, and separation of concerns.

nodes.label: Semantic HTML
nodes.rationale: Uses elements that convey meaning about content rather than appearance, improving accessibility, SEO, and code maintainability through clear landmarks for assistive technologies.

nodes.label: Semantic HTML5 Structural Elements
nodes.rationale: header, nav, main, article, section, aside, footer provide standardized vocabulary for webpage components and create implicit heading contexts.

nodes.label: Document Object Model (DOM)
nodes.rationale: HTML documents represented as tree-like structures that can be manipulated programmatically, enabling dynamic web applications.

nodes.label: DOCTYPE Declaration
nodes.rationale: <!DOCTYPE html> instructs the browser to render in standards mode, preventing quirks mode and ensuring consistent cross-browser behavior.

nodes.label: Meta Tags and Document Head
nodes.rationale: charset=UTF-8 ensures proper character encoding; viewport meta tag enables responsive scaling on mobile devices.

nodes.label: HTML Forms
nodes.rationale: Native HTML5 form features (input, select, textarea, fieldset, legend) provide structured user input with grouping and labeling for accessibility.

nodes.label: HTML5 Input Types
nodes.rationale: email, tel, url, number, date, color, range provide native browser validation and device-appropriate keyboards/controls.

nodes.label: HTML5 Form Validation Attributes
nodes.rationale: required, pattern, min, max, step, maxlength, novalidate enable client-side validation without JavaScript while supporting progressive enhancement.

nodes.label: HTML Accessibility (ARIA, WCAG 2.1)
nodes.rationale: Proper heading hierarchies, alt text, label/for relationships, ARIA landmarks and roles enhance assistive technology compatibility per WCAG 2.1.

nodes.label: Progressive Enhancement
nodes.rationale: Ensures basic functionality works with HTML alone, providing a resilient foundation that gracefully degrades and supports enhanced experiences when CSS/JS available.

nodes.label: Responsive Design
nodes.rationale: Integrates viewport and media capabilities to create adaptable interfaces across screen sizes and devices.

nodes.label: Search Engine Optimization (SEO)
nodes.rationale: Structured semantic data and heading hierarchy let search engines analyze, index, and understand content importance effectively.

nodes.label: HTML Multimedia Elements
nodes.rationale: audio, video, img, picture, figure/figcaption, iframe enable native media integration with cross-browser source formats, lazy loading, and fallbacks.

nodes.label: HTML Entities
nodes.rationale: Encode special characters (<, >, &, ©, currency, quotes) ensuring correct rendering across browsers and platforms.

nodes.label: Custom Data Attributes (data-*)
nodes.rationale: Store application-specific information on elements, supporting microdata and web components foundations while preserving semantics.

nodes.label: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets)
nodes.author: Sagar Thapa
nodes.rationale: Declarative stylesheet language using selectors, properties, and values to describe presentation and layout; separates visual presentation from HTML content structure.

nodes.label: CSS Box Model
nodes.rationale: Foundational concept governing element sizing/spacing via content, padding, border, and margin; box-sizing: border-box includes padding and border in dimensions.

nodes.label: Specificity and the Cascade
nodes.rationale: Establishes how style declarations are prioritized when multiple rules target an element, creating predictable hierarchies; efficient selectors avoid performance impact.

nodes.label: CSS Flexbox
nodes.rationale: One-dimensional layout system ideal for navigation bars and component arrangements; flex: 1 1 200px combines flex-grow, flex-shrink, flex-basis for responsive distribution and alignment.

nodes.label: CSS Grid
nodes.rationale: Two-dimensional layout system for page-level layouts; repeat(auto-fit, minmax(250px, 1fr)) creates responsive grids that adapt without media queries.

nodes.label: CSS Selectors
nodes.rationale: Class, pseudo-selectors (:hover, :focus), and efficient selector strategies provide predictable cascade behavior and target elements for styling.

nodes.label: CSS Custom Properties (Variables)
nodes.rationale: Defined at :root for global theming; enable dynamic theming, design tokens, and runtime style modifications through JavaScript integration.

nodes.label: Media Queries and Responsive Design
nodes.rationale: Mobile-first breakpoints (min-width 768px, 1200px), prefers-reduced-motion, and prefers-color-scheme adapt interfaces to viewport and user preferences.

nodes.label: Container Queries
nodes.rationale: container-type: inline-size and @container rules enable component-responsive design based on container size rather than viewport, supporting modular layouts.

nodes.label: clamp() Fluid Typography
nodes.rationale: clamp() scales typography smoothly between minimum and maximum values based on viewport, reducing media query complexity.

nodes.label: aspect-ratio Property
nodes.rationale: Provides proportional sizing (e.g. 16/9) maintaining visual consistency, eliminating the legacy padding-top percentage hack.

nodes.label: CSS Positioning
nodes.rationale: static, relative, absolute, fixed, sticky provide precise placement; sticky enables persistent headers and sidebars without JavaScript.

nodes.label: CSS Animations and Transitions
nodes.rationale: Animating transform and opacity triggers GPU acceleration for smooth performance; respects prefers-reduced-motion for accessibility.

nodes.label: CSS Architecture Methodologies (BEM, OOCSS, SMACSS)
nodes.rationale: Organizational frameworks addressing specificity management, component reusability, and naming conventions for scalable, maintainable CSS.

nodes.label: Mobile-First Methodology
nodes.rationale: Prioritizes performance/accessibility by establishing baseline functionality for constrained devices, then progressively enhancing for larger screens.

nodes.label: Separation of Concerns (HTML/CSS)
nodes.rationale: Separating content structure (HTML) from visual presentation (CSS) enables maintainable, scalable, accessible web applications.
